channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Enugu State to Build 660MW Coal Power Plant by 2028
item.description: Enugu State Government don unveil plan to construct one 660MW coal-fired power plant wey go start July this year. Governor Peter Mbah yan say this na part of im administration strategy to grow Enugu e...

item.title: Scottish Election Remains Unpredictable as SNP Faces Challenges from Disaffected Voters
item.description: As Scotland heads into one of its most unpredictable Holyrood elections in over a decade, the Scottish National Party (SNP) faces notable challenges from disillusioned supporters. Incidents during Joh...

item.title: Personal assistant of Suvendu Adhikari killed amid post-poll violence in West Bengal
item.description: In West Bengal, post-poll violence has taken a tragic turn with the killing of Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant, Chandranath Rath. The incident occurred on the night of May 6, 2026, in Madhyamgra...

item.title: Court hears details of N550 million Maitama property purchase in Yahaya Bello fraud trial
item.description: One prosecution witness, Shehu Bello, don appear for Federal High Court in Abuja to explain how one property for Maitama take get bought for N550 million as part of the ongoing fraud trial of former K...

item.title: COTU Responds to Gachagua’s Criticism of Atwoli, Defends Labour Movement Legacy
item.description: The Central Organisation of Trade Unions (COTU) has strongly responded to former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua after he criticized Secretary General Francis Atwoli. The spat began during the Natio...

item.title: Batangas lawmakers reject Leviste’s allegations against Recto couple, cite lack of evidence
item.description: Naglabas ng resolusyon ang ilang mambabatas mula sa Batangas na kumokondena sa mga alegasyon ni Batangas 1st District Representative Leandro Leviste laban kina Executive Secretary Ralph Recto at Batan...

item.title: Khana Youths Federation Collaborates with Police to Promote Peace and Youth Enterprise
item.description: For River State, youths don dey waka one big step for betterment as the Khana Local Government chapter of the Youth Federation join hands with Nigeria Police Force to boost peace and enterprise among ...

item.title: Developer Says KED Business Park Ready for Future Expansion
item.description: The article reports that the KED business park in Greater Sudbury is moving forward with new development plans despite concerns linked to the project’s previous developer. According to the current lea...

item.title: Poor funding leaves Nigeria’s foreign missions in bad shape, minister-designate warns
item.description: Nigeria minister of state-designate for foreign affairs, Sola Enikanolaiye, don raise serious concern about the bad condition of Nigerian missions abroad, blaming am mainly on lack of enough funding. ...

item.title: Dispute Between Akpabio and Oshiomhole Over Eligibility Rules for 11th Senate Leadership
item.description: Fresh gist don show why Senate President Godswill Akpabio and Senator Adams Oshiomhole from Edo North clash over the amended Senate standing rules. The new rules dey add extra conditions for who fit b...

item.title: Kelowna Curling Club suffers significant damage after planned rooftop break-in
item.description: The Kelowna Curling Club in downtown Kelowna has been targeted in a sophisticated break-in that caused extensive damage and resulted in stolen property. Early on Monday, at least two suspects climbed ...

item.title: Alix Earle Opens Up About Her Cosmetic Procedures and Experiences
item.description: Alix Earle, the American social media influencer and TikTok personality, has been open about her cosmetic procedures, sparking online discussions about beauty standards. She publicly confirmed having ...

item.title: Reflecting on the challenges of being a self-conscious parent in modern family life
item.description: In the second series of the BBC sitcom 'Amandaland', Lucy Punch returns as Amanda, an over-the-top and image-conscious mother navigating life in the trendy but less affluent area of South Harlesden. A...

item.title: Man seriously injured after alleged random stabbing in Dalby park
item.description: A 23-year-old man has been seriously injured following a stabbing incident in a public park in Dalby, a regional town west of Brisbane. The attack occurred on Wednesday afternoon at around 2:30pm whil...

item.title: Sir David Attenborough at 100: A century of inspiring love for nature
item.description: Sir David Attenborough turns 100 this week, celebrating an extraordinary seven-decade career with the BBC that has touched billions of lives. Very few people live for a century, and fewer still achiev...

item.title: PM Modi Commemorates First Anniversary of Operation Sindoor, Honours Armed Forces' Valour
item.description: On May 7, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi marked the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, praising the Indian armed forces for their exceptional courage and precision in responding to terrorist ...

item.title: Survey finds litter on nearly all main roads in Wales, volunteers highlight growing problem
item.description: A recent survey conducted by Keep Wales Tidy has revealed an alarming rise in roadside litter across Wales, with rubbish found on 98.8% of A and B roads. The most common items include sweet wrappers, ...

item.title: Mamela Nyamza Receives Standing Ovations at Baxter Theatre for Thought-Provoking Performances
item.description: Cape Town dancer and choreographer Mamela Nyamza recently performed a double bill at the Baxter Theatre, featuring her works 'Hatched Ensemble' and 'The Herd/Less'. The performances were met with stan...

item.title: Canucks Draft Outlook: Expert Advises Fans to Stay Positive Despite Lottery Setback
item.description: After a disappointing outcome in the NHL draft lottery, a Vancouver-based draft expert is encouraging Canucks fans to maintain optimism about the team’s prospects. While the lottery results may not ha...

item.title: MSF Highlights Severe Malnutrition Affecting Mothers and Infants in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders (MSF) has issued a detailed report accusing Israeli authorities of deliberately restricting food and humanitarian aid in Gaza, which has resulted in a serious malnutrition cris...
